title: Erigeron greenei
intro: Erigeron greenei, also known as Greene's fleabane, is a flowering plant species within the daisy family (Asteraceae). This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory activities. The bioactive constituents of Erigeron greenei include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Erigeron greenei include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
taxonomy: Erigeron greenei is classified in the kingdom Plantae, within the family Asteraceae, and in the genus Erigeron. The genus Erigeron belongs to the tribe Astereae and is a part of the subfamily Asteroideae. It is a flowering plant native to North America. The species Erigeron greenei is also known as Greene's fleabane. This classification reflects the plant's evolutionary relationships and its position within the plant kingdom.
